rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical Cp*Rh-catalyzed C-H alkenylation of 1-naphthols with alkenes under constant current in an undivided cell
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation regenerates the active Cp*Rh(III) catalyst from a reduced Rh species formed after C-H activation/alkene insertion/β-H elimination; H2 evolution at Pt cathode closes the redox balance

key_intermediates.name: Cp*Rh(III) naphtholate/aryl complex after C-H metalation
Rh(III)-alkyl after migratory insertion into alkene 2e
Reduced Rh species (Rh(I)) after β-hydride elimination/product release

electron_flow: Cp*Rh(III) performs C-H activation on 1-naphthol (non-rate-limiting), alkene inserts and β-H elimination delivers the alkenylated naphthol and a reduced Rh species; the anode reoxidizes Rh back to Rh(III) while protons are reduced to H2 at the Pt cathode.
rate_determining_step: Step after the C-H cleavage (likely migratory insertion or β-hydride elimination); C-H activation is not rate-limiting based on kH/kD ≈ 1.1

probe_reagents.name: [D]n-1a (ortho-deuterated 1-naphthol, 90% D)
probe_reagents.observation: Parallel initial-rate experiment gave kH/kD ≈ 1.1, indicating C-H cleavage is not rate-determining

notes: Only the KIE portion of the mechanistic studies is shown on this page; the near-unity KIE (1.1) argues against rate-limiting concerted metalation-deprotonation, suggesting a post-C-H-activation step controls the rate.
